name: elder-care-setup
description: Set up care for aging parents. Build an emergency plan, organize medications, structure the budget, create the care checklist, and establish a daily routine. For the sandwich generation trying to hold it all together.

config.instruction: Build an emergency plan for an aging parent: emergency contacts (doctors, hospital, pharmacy, neighbors), medical conditions and allergies, insurance information, legal documents location (POA, advance directive, will), and what to do in a crisis.
Organize all medications: name, dosage, frequency, prescribing doctor, pharmacy, interactions, refill dates. Create a daily medication schedule and a system for tracking compliance.

Budget for elder care: medical costs not covered by insurance, in-home care hours, assisted living evaluation, transportation, home modifications (grab bars, ramps), meal delivery, and respite care for the caregiver. Include what insurance/Medicare covers vs out-of-pocket.

Build the ongoing care checklist: weekly tasks (grocery, pharmacy, doctor appointments), monthly tasks (bill paying, insurance claims), quarterly tasks (care plan review, home safety check), and annual tasks (legal document review, insurance renewal).

Build a daily routine that balances caregiving with the caregiver's own life. Include: parent's needs schedule, caregiver's work, caregiver's self-care (non-negotiable), family coordination, and one hour that is entirely the caregiver's own. Burnout prevention is the priority.
